summary: PR 2603 split the Ice Climber/Popo Kirby special-N code out of `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` into a new `ftkirbyspecialiceclimber.c/.h`, adjusted split and symbol metadata, and marked both Mewtwo and Ice Climber Kirby special files as matching objects. The PR has no body, issue comments, review comments, or explicit validation output in the loaded slice.

central Kirby source now includes the new Ice Climber special header
no longer hosts unrelated Ice Climber/Popo Kirby special-N prototypes

what_changed.text: Created `ftkirbyspecialiceclimber.c` and moved the Ice Climber/Popo Kirby special-N implementation out of `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c`.
evidence_refs: diff: `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` removed the same family of functions after `ftKb_MtSpecialAirNEnd_Coll`

what_changed.text: Created `ftkirbyspecialiceclimber.h` and moved the `ftKb_SpecialNIc_*` and `ftKb_PpSpecial*` prototypes out of `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h`.
Updated split ranges so Mewtwo ends at 0x80108CE8, Ice Climber owns 0x80108CE8-0x80109260, and Yoshi begins at 0x80109260.
Changed affected sdata2 constants from global address-style `ftKb_Init_804D95xx` symbols to local `@NNN` labels.
Marked `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` as matching and added `ftkirbyspecialiceclimber.c` as matching in `configure.py`.
Cleaned include ownership in `ftkirby.c`, `ftkirby.h`, `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c`, and `itclimbersice.c` to follow the new file split.
evidence_refs: diff: include hunks in those files

smart_moves.text: The PR did not just move code; it also updated the split table, symbol metadata, configure matching status, and header includes needed for the new TU boundary.
Unrelated prototypes were removed from `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h` and placed in a more specific new Ice Climber special header.
The item file `itclimbersice.c` was updated to include the new owning header rather than depending on Donkey Kirby declarations.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a contiguous function range in one fighter special file actually belongs to another special-move family, a link/matching PR may need a coordinated TU split: move the functions, add an owning header, update split ranges, update symbol scopes, and update configure status together.
evidence_refs: diff: moved 0x80108CE8-era functions from Mewtwo file into new Ice Climber file

decomp_lessons.lesson: Header ownership should follow the function family after a TU split; avoid leaving prototypes in an unrelated special header only because previous code included them there.
scope: item includes that call Kirby callbacks
evidence_refs: diff: removed Ice Climber/Popo prototypes from `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h`

decomp_lessons.lesson: Local sdata2 labels can be part of a clean source split when constants belong to local TU-owned data rather than global address-named symbols.
scope: symbol metadata for split fighter TUs
evidence_refs: diff: `symbols.txt` changed 0x804D9508-0x804D9550 from global `ftKb_Init_*` to local `@NNN` labels

accepted_candidate_records.title: TU split for link/matching requires source, header, split, symbol, and configure updates
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2603 is evidence that when a function range is separated into a new fighter special TU, the reviewable change may need synchronized updates across source files, owning headers, `splits.txt`, `symbols.txt`, includes, and `configure.py` matching status.
